notes: Stratum prior vs stratum posterior; H = 0.5*ln(2πe) + ln(σ). Positive reduction = posterior concentrated (learning). KL capped at 1e6 (explodes when posterior_sigma << prior_sigma, which is expected when few feedback events dominate the conjugate update). Negative entropy reduction = posterior more uncertain than prior (sparse feedback).
Cause-level prior vs cause-level posterior for strata that fell back to cause prior. KL capped at 1e6.
Global prior (sigma=1.2394) vs global posterior (sigma=0.0582); n_eff_feedback=874.1.

CROSS-LEVEL AGGREGATE — not directly interpretable as a single comparison. Levels (stratum / cause / global) have different prior sigma distributions. H values are commensurable WITHIN a level only. Differential entropy H = 0.5*ln(2πe*σ²) can be negative when σ < 1/sqrt(2πe) ≈ 0.242.
